title: Hymenostephium guatemalense
intro: Hymenostephium guatemalense is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in Central America and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hymenostephium guatemalense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Hymenostephium guatemalense is classified under the plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Within this family, it is placed in the tribe Liabaeae. The genus Hymenostephium is further divided into several species, with H. guatemalense being one of the known species. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of H. guatemalense. The classification also enables researchers to group and study similar plant species within the same family or tribe.
